I will increase the spreader conductivity by a factor of 1.5 (from 90 to 135 W/(m*K)), which is within the allowed single-step ratio and bounds.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity enhances lateral heat spreading, reducing the local thermal resistance under the chip and lowering the peak temperature.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip_max_temperature towards or below 85.0°C
More uniform temperature distribution across the heat spreader
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if the bottleneck shifts to the base plate or boundary conditions rather than the spreader itself.

changes.reason: Increase spreader width by ~11% (within 1.5x limit) to maximize lateral heat spreading area towards the domain boundaries.
Increase spreader height by 50% (max allowed step) to increase vertical thermal cross-section and reduce resistance between chip and spreader.

risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if the bottleneck shifts entirely to the spreader or interface resistance
